mission.title: Install and Configure Akri on Kubernetes
mission.description: Akri is a Kubernetes Resource Interface for the Edge that allows you to expose heterogeneous leaf devices as resources in a Kubernetes cluster. Installing Akri enables you to utilize devices like IP cameras and USB devices seamlessly within your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the Akri Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the Akri Helm repository to your local Helm setup to access the Akri charts.
```bash
helm repo add akri https://project-akri.github.io/akri-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Akri using Helm
steps.description:
Now, install Akri in a dedicated namespace. This command will install Akri with the release name 'akri'.
```bash
helm install akri akri/akri --namespace akri --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Akri pods are running successfully by executing the following command:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace akri
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
To ensure optimal performance, configure resource limits for the Akri deployment. Edit the Akri deployment as follows:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment akri --namespace akri
```
In the editor, set the resource limits under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Akri
steps.description:
Create a Role and RoleBinding for Akri to ensure it has the necessary permissions:
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: akri
  name: akri-role
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods", "pods/status"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
EOF

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: akri-role-binding
  namespace: akri
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: akri-role
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: default
  namespace: akri
EOF
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Akri will show the Akri pods running in the 'akri' namespace. You can also verify that the resource limits and RBAC settings are correctly applied by checking the deployment and role bindings.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add akri https://project-akri.github.io/akri-charts
helm repo update

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, and that both Helm and kubectl are installed on your local machine.
